commands.description: Execute a one-line Python snippet directly from the CLI without creating a .py file, useful for quick automation or closing Blender after a task.
Programmatically save the current state of the Blender session to a specific file path during a script execution.
Launch Blender in background mode but open an interactive Python console, allowing for real-time debugging and inspection of the data API.
Safely access a collection by name using .get() to prevent script crashes if the collection does not exist (returns None instead of raising KeyError).
Launch Blender with factory settings, ignoring all user preferences and add-ons. Essential for debugging script behavior in a clean environment.
